questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: In the context of the 'Tribal Village Vision 2030' Declaration, what does the term 'Adi Sewa Samay' signify?

options.text: A fixed duration for the Gram Sabha to meet and finalize the Village Development Action Plan.
A mandatory period of one hour per week where villagers contribute voluntary service.
The operational hours of the single-window Adi Sewa Kendras in tribal villages.
The time-bound grievance redressal mechanism for Forest Rights Act claims.

questions.explanation: Adi Sewa Samay is a specific feature of the Tribal Village Vision 2030 where villagers contribute 1 hour per week of voluntary service to foster community participation and development.
questions.takeaway: Adi Sewa Samay is a community-driven voluntary service component of the Tribal Village Vision 2030.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Adi Sewa Samay: Villagers contribute 1 hour/week of voluntary service.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: The 'Adi Sewa Kendras', mentioned in the context of tribal governance, are primarily intended to serve as:

options.text: Residential schools for tribal children to improve literacy rates.
Single-window service centres for effective delivery of government schemes in villages.
Marketing hubs for Minor Forest Produce (MFP) collected by tribal cooperatives.
Specialized courts for the adjudication of disputes under the Forest Rights Act.

questions.explanation: Adi Sewa Kendras are established as an institutional mechanism under the Tribal Village Vision 2030 to act as single-window service centres in every village.
questions.takeaway: Adi Sewa Kendras aim to streamline service delivery and financial/social inclusion at the village level through a single-window system.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Institutional mechanism: Adi Sewa Kendras in every village as single-window service centres.
